title: WIN-014 / SEC-6.14: PRED-TIER-3 implied ΔB band made explicit (16.8–21.8 nT) — closes ISS-1957 orphan
target: data/sections.json:part6.html (SEC-6.14 vise paragraph) + data/wins.json:WIN-014 detail_evidence — two narrow precision patches making the implied August 2026 ΔB premise explicit. The dome publishes a 10–13 µGal SG-pulse target (PRED-TIER-3) but no quantitative ΔB premise. Through κ = 1.677 nT/µGal, the 10–13 µGal target back-implies an unstated August 2026 ΔB band of 16.8–21.8 nT at the named target stations — at or above the upper edge of the Kim & Chang 2018 global eclipse-magnetic ensemble typical totality range of 5–15 nT. Patch makes the back-calc explicit, addresses the defender pivot 'vise arithmetic is non-obvious without knowing assumed ΔB', and strengthens the cross-eclipse vise by showing the dome must commit to an anomalously high ΔB premise to keep the 10–13 µGal target arithmetically self-consistent with its own κ.
review_class_override_note: ISS-1957 carries class_hint='verification' from operator-manual-reroute on 2026-05-12, but PROP-025 author authority is invoked to declare review_class='deep-attack'. Justification: the work adds a new explicit derivation step (10–13 µGal × κ = 16.8–21.8 nT implied ΔB band) and a new comparison (vs Kim & Chang 5–15 nT ensemble) that did not appear in the prior text. While the structural vise argument is preserved, the patch strengthens it by closing the 'unstated ΔB premise' defender pivot — that is argument-strengthening work characteristic of deep-attack, not a mechanical value-fact-check characteristic of verification. Comparable in shape to EXP-383 (single-target singleton resolving a major-severity orphan ISS).
orphan_rescue_note: ISS-1957 was created 2026-05-11 with status='assigned-analyst' via operator-manual-reroute on 2026-05-12T05-30 (routed_by_run='operator-manual-rereoute-2026-05-12T05-30') without writing an expansion-tracker entry. The previous Opus analyst run (2026-05-14T16:10:46.654Z) detected the orphan via PROP-029 safety net but LEFT it as orphan ('class_hint=verification + severity=major edge case'). Per analyst.md dispatcher rule, severity=major orphans are Opus-owned. This run picks it up; the prior orphan attention-inbox notice (PROP029-ORPHAN-DETECTED-2026-05-14) covers tinker investigation of the operator-manual-reroute / tracker-write decoupling — no new attention-inbox entry is needed this run.
summary_for_decider:
EXP-384 closes ISS-1957 (orphan, major severity, WIN-014.c7 H1) with two narrow precision patches that make explicit the implied August 2026 ΔB premise. The dome publishes a 10–13 µGal PRED-TIER-3 SG-pulse target; through κ = 1.677 nT/µGal this back-implies a ΔB band of 16.77–21.80 nT at the named target stations — at or above the upper edge of the 5–15 nT global eclipse-magnetic typical totality ensemble (Kim & Chang 2018, already cited in WIN-014 + SEC-6.14). PATCH-A modifies SEC-6.14:part6.html vise paragraph to add one explicit-arithmetic sentence and acknowledge the typical-ensemble-ΔB-yields-only-6-7.8 µGal counter-arithmetic (the kernel of truth the ISS-1957 author raised). PATCH-B modifies WIN-014.detail_evidence to add a brief parenthetical near the existing '10–13 µGal target' reference, pointing to the back-calc. Both anchors uniqueness-verified (single match in each file). Recommended action: PATCH the two locations; close ISS-1957 as fixed-pending-verification on the post-build curmudgeon verification pass.

Review class declared deep-attack (overriding class_hint='verification' from operator-manual-reroute) per PROP-025: the patches add an explicit new derivation step and a new comparison vs ensemble, closing the 'unstated ΔB premise' defender pivot. Singleton-shaped, comparable to EXP-383.

Not conflicting with the open EXP-349 (different ISS-1959, different paragraph in WIN-014 about Chinese SG totality coverage). Not conflicting with EXP-364 (different SEC-6.14 paragraph; PATCH-364-A reframed the H7 derivation language, this patch extends with explicit numeric back-calc downstream in the vise paragraph).

decision.rationale: Option A (explicit back-calc + ensemble comparison + counter-arithmetic acknowledgment) is correct because: (1) ISS-1957 specifically asks the dome's 10–13 µGal range be derived from dome parameters and shown achievable — the back-calc 10 µGal × 1.677 nT/µGal = 16.77 nT, 13 µGal × 1.677 nT/µGal = 21.80 nT is the only published-parameter derivation possible given κ is the dome's only published mechanism producing the SG-pulse target; (2) the counter-arithmetic (10–13 nT through κ yields 5.96–7.75 µGal, below the target) is the kernel of truth the ISS author raised — directly acknowledging it strengthens rather than weakens our position because it transparently makes the dome's ΔB premise the load-bearing claim; (3) the Kim & Chang 5–15 nT ensemble comparison is already cited in both WIN-014 and SEC-6.14, so the ensemble-comparison sentence introduces no new external citation and lands the back-calc in a context the reader has already encountered; (4) the patch is non-circular: it reads the dome's parameters (κ = 1.677, target = 10–13 µGal) and computes — does not retrofit any claim to data. Forward-looking: when the dome publishes a quantitative August 2026 ΔB premise (either in advance or after the eclipse), our patch's 'unstated premise' framing becomes either confirmed (dome's premise falls inside 16.8–21.8 nT and is anomalous) or rebutted (dome's premise falls inside 5–15 nT and the κ derivation is mathematically inconsistent with the 10–13 µGal target — a different but equally strong refutation). Symmetric falsification preserved.
decision.why_not_option_b: Option B (close ISS-1957 with no patch on the grounds that PATCH-364-A already reframed the H7 derivation language as 'natural reading + we have not located') was considered but rejected because: (i) PATCH-364-A reframed the EPISTEMIC STATUS of the κ-pathway claim (assertion → inference) but did NOT make the implied ΔB band quantitatively explicit — the 'how much ΔB does 10–13 µGal require' question remains unaddressed in the current text, which is exactly what ISS-1957 flagged; (ii) the defender pivot 'vise arithmetic is non-obvious without knowing assumed ΔB' is still live; (iii) the kernel-of-truth standard requires acknowledging the ISS author's counter-arithmetic (10–13 nT → 6.0–7.8 µGal) head-on rather than dismissing it via 'we already reframed the language'; (iv) leaving ISS-1957 unresolved keeps the major-severity orphan in the queue indefinitely.
decision.why_not_option_c: Option C (declare review_class='verification' and treat as baby-batchable since the work is mechanical arithmetic) was considered but rejected because: (i) the new derivation step (explicit 16.8–21.8 nT band) and the new comparison (vs ensemble) are argument-strengthening additions, not mechanical fact-checks of existing values; (ii) the patch closes a defender pivot — that is deep-attack scope; (iii) treating as verification would route to baby's batched curmudgeon review, but the patch's load-bearing assertion ('the implied ΔB band sits at or above the typical ensemble upper edge') warrants singleton adversarial review to test the 'maybe the ensemble doesn't apply to August 2026' counter-pivot. Singleton review is the conservative choice.

patches.target_path: part6.html — SEC-6.14 (#p6-614-kappa-cluster) vise paragraph, insertion after existing 'no derivation' sentence and before 'Both horns lose at least one WIN'
patches.uniqueness_verified: Anchor 'Both horns lose at least one WIN. This is the structural argument that anchors WIN-013, WIN-014, and the verdict-tier of the κ-cluster more generally.' appears exactly once in data/sections.json (count = 1, verified via node split).
patches.find: The dome publishes κ as a constant; either it is, in which case 1999/2009 refute it, or it is not, in which case the 10–13 µGal SG-pulse number has no derivation. Both horns lose at least one WIN. This is the structural argument that anchors WIN-013, WIN-014, and the verdict-tier of the κ-cluster more generally.
patches.replace: The dome publishes κ as a constant; either it is, in which case 1999/2009 refute it, or it is not, in which case the 10–13 µGal SG-pulse number has no derivation. Note further: reading the 10–13 µGal target through κ inversely — Δg × κ = ΔB — back-implies an unstated August 2026 ΔB premise of 16.8–21.8 nT at the named target stations (10 µGal × 1.677 nT/µGal = 16.77 nT; 13 µGal × 1.677 nT/µGal = 21.80 nT). This implied band sits at or above the upper edge of the global eclipse-magnetic typical totality ensemble (5–15 nT per Kim & Chang 2018, the same 207-measurement ensemble the dome itself invokes for WIN-068). A defender who assumes the dome takes a typical-ensemble ΔB of 10–13 nT for August 2026 finds that 10/1.677 ≈ 6.0 µGal and 13/1.677 ≈ 7.8 µGal — both below the 10–13 µGal SG-pulse target. The arithmetic is internally consistent only if the dome commits to a ΔB premise above the typical ensemble; that premise itself is unpublished in any dome material we have located. Either the dome publishes its quantitative August 2026 ΔB prediction in advance (in which case the κ derivation becomes auditable in advance — a symmetric strengthening of the vise) or it does not (in which case the 10–13 µGal target's derivation has an additional unspecified premise beyond κ). Both horns lose at least one WIN. This is the structural argument that anchors WIN-013, WIN-014, and the verdict-tier of the κ-cluster more generally.
patches.rationale: Closes ISS-1957 H1 by making the implied August 2026 ΔB band explicit (16.77–21.80 nT) via κ-inverse back-calculation from the dome's own published 10–13 µGal SG-pulse target. Acknowledges the kernel-of-truth counter-arithmetic the ISS author raised (10–13 nT through κ yields 5.96–7.75 µGal, below target) head-on rather than dismissing it. The added text places the back-calc inside the vise paragraph in a single insertion (no rearrangement of the existing four sentences around 'Both horns lose at least one WIN'). New citation: none — Kim & Chang 2018 (DOI 10.1016/j.asr.2018.01.022) is already cited earlier in this same section ('What κ predicts during past eclipses'). Maintains symmetric-falsification framing: a quantitative August 2026 ΔB publication strengthens rather than weakens the vise.

patches.target_path: WIN-014.detail_evidence — last paragraph, insertion after 'the 10–13 µGal target' reference (single occurrence in detail_evidence; uniqueness verified for cross-file context)
patches.uniqueness_verified: Anchor 'because κ is the only published mechanism producing the 10–13 µGal target — addressed as a class in the κ-cluster section's vise subsection.' appears exactly once in data/wins.json (count = 1, verified via node split).
patches.find: because κ is the only published mechanism producing the 10–13 µGal target — addressed as a class in the κ-cluster section's vise subsection.
patches.replace: because κ is the only published mechanism producing the 10–13 µGal target (which, through κ = 1.677 nT/µGal, back-implies an unstated August 2026 ΔB premise of 16.8–21.8 nT at the named target stations — at or above the upper edge of the global eclipse-magnetic typical totality ensemble of 5–15 nT cited for WIN-068) — addressed as a class in the κ-cluster section's vise subsection.
patches.rationale: Closes ISS-1957 H1 from the WIN-014 detail_evidence side: adds a single parenthetical to the existing PRED-TIER-3 cross-reference making the implied ΔB band explicit. Mirrors the SEC-6.14 patch in compressed form (one parenthetical clause rather than a full insertion). The 5–15 nT ensemble figure is already cited in this same paragraph of WIN-014.detail_evidence three sentences earlier ('typical totality magnetic perturbations of order 5–15 nT at central observatories' — Kim & Chang 2018), so this insertion introduces no new external citation.

defender_pivots_addressed.pivot: The 10–13 µGal target isn't derived via κ at all — it's a direct dome prediction from some other mechanism.
defender_pivots_addressed.rebuttal: Acknowledged in advance via 'unstated August 2026 ΔB premise' framing. The section already states 'we have located no alternative derivation in the dome's published material' (PATCH-364-A). The back-calc is the natural reading of the dome's parameters; if a non-κ derivation exists, the dome should publish it. Symmetric falsification preserved — any future dome publication of a non-κ derivation can be tested against this patch's prediction.

defender_pivots_addressed.pivot: Kim & Chang 5–15 nT is across many eclipses; individual events can exceed.
defender_pivots_addressed.rebuttal: Acknowledged via 'at or above the upper edge' language — does not claim 16.8–21.8 nT is impossible, only that it sits at the upper edge of the typical totality range. The implied premise is anomalous, not impossible. The vise rests on the premise being unstated, not on it being unattainable.

defender_pivots_addressed.pivot: The 2026 eclipse path might have anomalously high ΔB for site-specific or solar-cycle reasons.
defender_pivots_addressed.rebuttal: Possibly true; this is precisely what the patch asks the dome to publish in advance. The 'either the dome publishes its quantitative August 2026 ΔB prediction in advance or it does not' framing accommodates this — a published premise of 16.8–21.8 nT (with site-specific or solar-cycle justification) renders the κ derivation auditable in advance, which is exactly the symmetric-falsification standard the κ-cluster section invokes.

defender_pivots_addressed.pivot: The vise arithmetic is non-obvious without knowing assumed ΔB. (This is the defender pivot ISS-1957 specifically calls out.)
defender_pivots_addressed.rebuttal: Directly closed by the patch — the implicit ΔB premise is now explicit (16.77–21.80 nT), making the arithmetic transparent. The 'non-obvious' character of the prior vise framing is no longer available as a defender retreat.

kernel_of_truth.acknowledged: The ISS-1957 author's counter-arithmetic — that 10–13 nT (the typical ensemble band) through κ yields 5.96–7.75 µGal, below the 10–13 µGal target — is CORRECT. Far from refuting our position, this arithmetic is exactly what makes the vise tight: the dome's PRED-TIER-3 commitment is only self-consistent with κ if the dome simultaneously commits to a ΔB premise above the typical ensemble. The patch makes this commitment explicit and load-bearing rather than implicit and deniable.
kernel_of_truth.why_does_not_save_dome: Acknowledging the counter-arithmetic does not rescue the dome because (a) the dome publishes no quantitative August 2026 ΔB premise that would justify an above-ensemble ΔB; (b) any post-eclipse publication of such a premise is a post-hoc carve-out, not a model output; (c) the cross-eclipse vise still applies regardless of which ΔB band the dome eventually commits to — either κ couples 1999/2009 (refuted by SG nulls) or κ has scope conditions (which destroy PRED-TIER-3's derivation). The back-calc strengthens the vise; it does not weaken it.

next_steps_for_decider: Apply two find_replace patches against data/sections.json and data/wins.json. No code changes; no test changes; no JS rebuild required for HTML (sections.json drives part6 rendering at build time). On successful build, close ISS-1957 as fixed-pending-verification. Update expansion-tracker.json with EXP-384 entry (status: pending → ready-for-integration → integrated per decider workflow; tracker entry to be self-written by this analyst run per orphan rescue procedure since no decider-authored tracker entry exists). No attention-inbox notice needed (prior PROP029-ORPHAN-DETECTED-2026-05-14 still pending and covers the tracker-write decoupling).
